clawkeys_protocol.specification: OWASP-compliant key identification and access control framework

human_keys.name: ClawKeys©™ (Human User Keys)
human_keys.purpose: User identity verification (one key per user, unchanged)
human_keys.storage: Hashed with SHA-256 in users.key_hash (database column)
human_keys.comparison: Query-based matching (SELECT * FROM users WHERE key_hash = ?) — no timing oracle
human_keys.revocation: Only via account deletion or manual admin action

lobster_keys.name: LobsterKeys©™ (API Service Keys)
lobster_keys.purpose: Machine identity for external integrations and agents
lobster_keys.storage: Hash (api_key_hash) + ShellCrypted value (api_key) in lobster_keys table

rate_limit.enforcement: lobsterRateLimiter middleware (in-memory counter store with LRU eviction, max 100 keys)
rate_limit.implementation: Window-based counters (60-second rolling window per key, lazy reset on next request)
rate_limit.zero_handling: 0 treated as unlimited (no rate limiting applied)

lobster_keys.revocation: Set is_active = 0 (soft delete, subsequent auth attempts fail)

session_tokens.name: Session Tokens (API Token)
session_tokens.purpose: Ephemeral authentication tokens for HTTP Bearer auth
session_tokens.storage: Plain text key in api_tokens table (short TTL justifies no hashing)
session_tokens.owner_type: human | lobster (key can be issued on behalf of human or lobster key)
session_tokens.expires_at: ISO 8601 timestamp, auto-delete on lookup if past expiry
session_tokens.revocation: DELETE from api_tokens (logout action or session expiry cleanup)

rejection_sampling.unbiased_generation: Ensures no modulo bias in random character selection

shellcryption_protocol.specification: AES-256-GCM encryption with HKDF key derivation and authenticated data

key_derivation.use_case_separation: Different salt per field type (notes, lobster_keys, api_tokens)

encryptField.operation: Single field encryption (used for note content, lobster api_key)
encryptField.input: Plain text string and field identifier
encryptField.output: Base64-encoded ciphertext with embedded nonce + auth_tag
encryptField.aad: field_name used as Additional Authenticated Data

decryptField.operation: Single field decryption with tamper detection
decryptField.output: Plain text string OR '[Decryption Failed]' marker
decryptField.failure_handling: Returns marker string on auth tag failure (tamper detection)

encryptRecord.output: Same structure with content field encrypted
encryptRecord.use_case: Storing encrypted note content in database

decryptRecord.output: Decrypted note object (or content = '[Decryption Failed]' if tamper detected)
decryptRecord.use_case: Retrieving decrypted note content from database

authentication_vector.authenticated_data: field_name or record metadata used as AAD
authentication_vector.purpose: Prevent decryption of field in wrong context
authentication_vector.tamper_detection: Auth tag mismatch → decryption returns marker, not error
authentication_vector.risk_mitigated: Swap attacks (encrypted note content moved to different field)

storage_integration.note_content: Encrypted via encryptRecord on INSERT, decrypted on SELECT
storage_integration.lobster_api_key: Encrypted via encryptField, stored as TEXT in lobster_keys.api_key
storage_integration.api_tokens: Stored plaintext (short TTL = acceptable risk, auto-deleted after 24h)
storage_integration.environment_requirement: SHELL_ENCRYPTION_PASSWORD must be set before any encrypt/decrypt operations

failure_modes.wrong_password: Auth tag verification fails → returns '[Decryption Failed]' marker
failure_modes.tampered_ciphertext: Auth tag verification fails → returns '[Decryption Failed]' marker
failure_modes.missing_nonce: Malformed base64 → JSON parse error or decryption fails → marker returned
failure_modes.null_plaintext: Graceful handling of null/undefined inputs (no crash)

verification.performance: AES-256-GCM hardware acceleration on modern CPUs

auth_flow.step_1_register: User provides hu- ClawKey → stored as SHA-256 key_hash in users table
auth_flow.step_2_token_issue: User proves possession of hu- ClawKey → server issues api- session token (32-char base62)
auth_flow.step_3_api_requests: User includes 'Bearer api-<token>' in Authorization header
auth_flow.step_4_token_lookup: Middleware extracts token → query api_tokens table → verify not expired
auth_flow.step_5_token_cleanup: Expired tokens deleted on lookup (soft cleanup) or on logout (explicit)
auth_flow.step_6_agent_auth: External agent uses lb- LobsterKey → server creates api- token from lb- key_hash

permission_enforcement.human_users: All permissions granted (canRead/Write/Edit/Delete = true by default)
permission_enforcement.lobster_agents: Permissions stored as JSON in lobster_keys.permissions → checked by requirePermission()
permission_enforcement.example: LobsterKey with {canRead: true, canWrite: false} → blocks PUT/PATCH to notes
